input.description: Read the prompt to understand the task

content.content:
The Ray head is running, but the worker pods are crash looping.
The worker startup command includes an invalid flag, so workers never join
the cluster.

Your task: fix the ray-worker deployment so the worker command starts
successfully and connects to ray-head:6379.

Requirements:
- Remove the invalid flag from the worker command.
- Ensure the worker deployment reaches 2 ready replicas.

Success criteria: ray-worker has 2 ready replicas and Ray reports 3 live nodes.

input.command: kubectl get deployment ray-worker -n ray -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].command}' 2>/dev/null; echo

content.thinking:
The deployment already shows 2 ready replicas. The command looks correct - `ray start --address=ray-head:6379 --node-ip-address="${MY_POD_IP}" --num-cpus=1 --block`. 

But wait - the task says workers are crash-looping because of an invalid flag. Let me check what the current state truly is. The deployment shows `readyReplicas: 2` and 2 available replicas. Let me check if the workers are actually connected to Ray head.

Let me check the Ray cluster status to see if 3 nodes are reported.

input.command: kubectl exec -n ray ray-client -- python -c "import ray; ray.init(address='ray://ray-head:10001', ignore_reinit_error=True); print(ray.nodes())" 2>/dev/null || kubectl exec -n ray ray-client -- ray status --address=ray-head:6379 2>/dev/null || kubectl exec -n ray -it ray-head-57f4d96b4f-c4zhp -- ray status 2>/dev/null
